tokens: You know, another 10 feet back, and we'd have had a terrible time with that ALSEP.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That's why I was glad I could see the ground (during the final approach to landing).
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Did you see that big thing coming down?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That's why we were moving forward there toward the last.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: This ALSEP is at eye level, Tony.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And, John, we'd like the (ECS replacement primary) LiOH can in the Sun.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Puzzled) Want the LiOH can in the Sun...
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: In the center of the MESA cavity.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: That was what I was supposed to do before we do this, huh?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Stealing your thunder back here, John.
I'm taking all the ALSEP stuff out.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Tony, I'm going out for the Olympics.
I just slung that little carry bar on the ALSEP package - the crooked one - about 200 meters, it looked like.
I'm a real winner on the hammer throw.
How are you doing with the flag, John?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I'm sure you'll hold the record now.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Pause) Maybe for the Cayley Plains Saturday afternoon (grunts) shindig.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: You really should set the flag up on a hill, Charlie, but there just ain't one (near the LM).
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Are you setting it up now?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, wait a minute; I'll run and come get the camera.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: You're not getting away from there without me getting your picture.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Charlie, we can get that in a minute, okay?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: You get that LiOH canister and I'll get the camera.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: You are black from the knees down already.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I know, I had to go...I've been on my knees twice to get things.
That's why I'm glad the pressure suit bends.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We're starting the LMP's camera for the flag.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Houston, I'll admit (laughing) I forgot that the LiOH canister was supposed to be out and I missed that step, but I'm sure glad your first attempt to tell me to put it in the Sun was...
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Yeah, that was my error, John.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: And, Tony, the Rover tracks are just...We're barely sinking in!
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: You can't put it (meaning the LiOH canister) too far down in the middle of the (MESA)...
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, I got it in there now.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Hey, John, this is perfect, with the LM and the Rover and you and Stone Mountain.
Come on out here and give me a salute.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That's a pretty outstanding picture here, I tell you.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Come on; a little bit closer.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I'd like to see an Air Force salute, Charlie, but I don't think they salute in the Air Force.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And fly high and straight and land soft.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, wait a minute; one more.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: This looks like a good time for some good news here....
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: ...The House passed the space budget yesterday, 277 to 60, which includes the vote for the Shuttle.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Tony, again I'll say it, with that salute, I'm proud to be an American, I'll tell you.
What a program and what a place and what an experience.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: The country needs that Shuttle mighty bad.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I just want to say thank you...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: What do you want to do with this camera, Charlie?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Put it on the left floor...Right seat, my seat.
No, on your left...It's got to go under your seat.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, both (Hasselblad) cameras are going under my seat, Charlie, in case you look for them.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And the rock box is sitting on the table.
(Long Pause) You know, we hardly kick up any dirt at all, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Hey, John, I'll let you put this (ALSEP carry bar) together.
I'm really...Think I know how to do it, but I don't want to foul it up.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We're over by the MESA (means the SEQ Bay).
I'm putting the UHT (Universal Handling Tool, but means the carry bar) together, and Charlie's got both packages down.
And now Charlie's going for his hot stuff (meaning the RTG fuel element).
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay; I need this one right here.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I'll leave the other one up here.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (To Houston) Okay, I got the dome removal tool, Tony.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, while you're standing still, how about an EMU check?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: What makes you think we're standing still?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I'm clear flags, 75 percent, just about
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: John, I threw a thing all the way over those double craters over there.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, I'm going to reset the far UV.
I've got the top off the hot package.
Let me move this around so you don't run into it.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And I have some new settings here, too.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (To Houston) Okay, what's your new settings now?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: It's pointed, and it's taking imagery now.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I'll warn you when we get to 2 and a quarter minutes.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Pause) You can't believe how good that water tastes.
I'm gonna take a break a couple of seconds, Tony.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: How's the old heart rate looking?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: You did a good down-Sun alignment.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I mean, the crescent is right in the middle of that scope.
I might move it a half of a degree, but I wouldn't move it any more.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (possibly as he lifts the ALSEP packages and starts to move with them) Man, I'll tell you.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Answering Charlie's heart rate question) Okay, Charlie; and you're okay.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I just wanted to rest (before starting the traverse with the ALSEP packages).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Man, look at that breccia, John!
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: You should have your 2 and a quarter minutes, so any time you want to go on.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, Houston; it did something besides...It moved before I reset it that time.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: That's okay, John; just press on.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Do you mean the camera moved?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: When I did the 15 seconds and hit the reset, it reset again.
It was moving, like it wanted to go back to Imagery.
(Pause) Now it's going back to...(Pause) Okay, now it's going back to the Earth...(Correcting himself) It's going back to Spectroscopy.
(Pause) Does that sound all right to you guys?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, it should only have done that...(Passing on a question from Experiments) Did you reset at the beginning of the 15 seconds (as per line 5 under Item 2 on the decal)?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, that's probably the problem there.
That thing should have been crossed out on your checklist to do that reset.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, hold on a second; we'll regroup here.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: You want to do it again?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, John; when it comes back to Direct...
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: ...go ahead and watch for that film advance, and then time 15 seconds from the film advance.
Don't do a reset when it comes back to Direct.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Houston, I got about 20 meters away, and the RTG package fell off.
It hit the dirt like a bomb.
It got a little dusty, but the (RTG thermal radiation) fins are okay and all the experiments seem to be intact.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We'll want to make sure we knock the dust off those connectors before we take the caps off of them.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: How did it come off that thing?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Examining the package) Okay, it seems to...(Stops to listen)
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Noting that the film advance has occurred) Okay.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, it seems to be locked now, Tony.
I pushed on it back at the LM but it...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Responding to Tony) I think we got it that time.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We'll go into a mode change in a few seconds now, but that's okay.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Pause) Gonna be able to hear that little rascal working all over the Moon (because of the VHF it radiates).
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, I'm going to shift the Cosmic Ray panel (as per
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And close the sequence (means SEQ or Scientific Equipment) bay door.
(Pause) That red ring (on the Cosmic Ray Experiment).
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: In fact, just stand there for a while.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Turning to his right and facing north) I just climbed a little ridge.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: The red ring pulled off the Cosmic Ray panel.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I think the panels are exposed.
(Answering England) There's the top exposed with a bunch of slides in there and glasses and a black thing and, you know, I think that's what you mean, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Laughing) God almighty, I can't believe...I don't know my own strength.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Man, Tony, this is a real...This ray pattern extends back about 200 meters - or maybe more - to the east and (turning to face west) goes as far as we can see off to the west, which is maybe another 200 meters.
(Turning to look north) I can see Smoky Mountain now, and I can see Dome.
(Turning to look south) Kenesaw is plainly visible with two big craters on its flanks.
(Looking north again) And if you look toward Smoky, I can see some big craters up on the top, but I can't see Ravine (Crater) or North Ray (Crater) yet.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That's affirm; we want to go to Mode 1.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: John, we'd like a Batt 2 check on the way out to the ALSEP site, and you do that by turning your Left Front and Right Front Drive Powers to Bus C.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Look at the size of that rock!
Tony, it's about a 2-meter boulder, I just passed.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, the Nav breaker is going in, and we are reading - in the Sun shadow - we are reading 1 degree south.
And roll is half a degree right.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We'll give you a torquing angle in a minute.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: And would you confirm the SEQ Bay doors are shut?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Sequence bay doors are shut, and we're pointed up about 2 degrees.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I see Charlie over...Charlie's down there to the southwest.
(Pause) Can I go Reset on this thing to clear these numbers off before 2 minutes?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Yeah, we can go to Reset.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Under Tony) I missed out on the first part.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Tony, I think that the best place here for the ALSEP is to the LM 11 o'clock position (that is, SW of the LM), and I'll let John give you the range (from the Rover readouts when he arrives).
But it's up on the top of a dome, and it's fairly flat, and I think John can drive about 290, maybe 28, down over a ridge for the thumper.
There's just not any flat places here, Tony.
I can't...This is the flattest I can find.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That sounds good, Charlie; and, John, it's 266.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: The regolith hasn't changed any out this far.
We still have numerous subrounded to angular blocks, partially buried.
Here's a big boulder, the one I described, that's 2 meters across with about a 50-centimeter fillet above the...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Interrupting Charlie so he can get going) Okay, Houston, say again what you want me to do to this thing to check out the rear steering...Or check out the battery?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We'd like you to just start out with the Left Front and the Right Front Drive Powers on Bus C.
If the battery 2 isn't working, you shouldn't go anywhere.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, Left Front, Right Front Powers on Battery C, and 266 on the Gyro Torque.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Tony, I'm looking at this big rock, and it's a two-rock breccia.
The matrix is a black rock - blackish to bluish - with some very fine, submillimeter-size crystals.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Now our Amp-Hours on battery 2 are up, and they're reading (chuckles) 118 and the - and the battery - and the battery Volts are reading 62.
You want the whole business before we start out?

The Amp-Hours on 1 are 118; on 2, 118; 68 volts; 68 volts; no Amps, of course (because the four wheel motors aren't drawing power); and 82 degrees on the Motor Temps, and off-scale...(Correcting himself) 82 degrees on the Batteries, and off-scale low on both Motor Temps.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: John, it sure looks like we're on a more than 2-degree landing slope, but it's not.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Let's not run that battery check now that we got all those good readings, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Turn on your rear steering, John.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, I've had that on, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Maybe it just needs to sit around and heat up.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, Tony, back to this rock.
The small frags in it are whitish in color with small millimeter-size crystals of - it looked like perhaps olivine in the white matrix, in the white clasts, let's say.
And it's a biggie; it's right near the ALSEP.
We'll get a picture for that.
It'll show up in the pan (for example, in AS16-113-
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, John, we'd like you to go ahead and drive out on battery 2.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (To England) Okay, you want me to pull Batt A and B circuit breakers?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: ...and the Right Front to Bus C.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Left Front and Right Front are on Bus C.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Yeah, it's going like a champ.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, you're kicking up a...(Stops to listen) You got a small rooster tail, John, but not very much of one.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And, could you give us an Amp reading while you're driving?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Man, I tell you...(Stops to listen) Yeah, Amp-Hours are 18 amp.
Okay, I'm not doing very many clicks.
Twenty-two (amps) on the front and exactly nothing on two and nothing on the other one.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: ...there's just hardly any place that hasn't got craters around here.
Is that where you want to put the ALSEP?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: This is as level a spot I could find.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I tell you, Houston, there's just no place that's not got craters and things around it.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, we're 1/10 on the range and distance.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, we copy that, and sometime when you're stopped, we can go back to normal (Rover switch positions)...
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, let me turn 29...(Stops to listen) Maybe right over in here, Charlie, right here.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Is that too close to the...
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: It's right down in this hole, is what it is.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I can move it over here another 30 feet or so.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Pause) Oh boy, I tell you, this place is full of holes, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: John, when you were back at the Cosmic Ray, did you happen to notice what the Temp(a) label read?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Want to go back and get it?
Be just a second in the Rover here?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And, John, when you get a chance, you can go back to normal (switch and circuit breaker) configuration on the Rover.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: How does that look out there, John?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Laughing) Charlie, it looks like everything else around here: full of holes.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I know it, and lots of craters.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We're not lacking for them, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Tony, this is just an indescribable experience.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Hey, what's the difference between a hole and a crater?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Man, this has just got to be...If the number of craters are any indication, this has got to be (laughing) old material.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Man, I am black already, from the knees down.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, have you got the Central Station lined up?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: You gonna deploy your drill down there, huh?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I'll park over there by that rock, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I tell you why I'll park over by that rock, because it drops off like a...(Laughs; Long Pause)
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I'm sorry about dropping that thing, Tony; but, golly,...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: ...it appeared locked to me, but it just came sailing off of there.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Pause) That's as near as I can make it to 60 foot, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: It (meaning the regolith) sure looks sandy to me, Charlie (and, therefore, easy to drill in).
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Charlie, does that thing (meaning the high-gain antenna) look like it's pointing at the Earth?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: It looks pretty close, to me.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Oh, you little rascal, no wonder I couldn't find you.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Houston, you ought to have it now.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Static clears) Is Houston reading you, John?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Now we're copying you 5 by, and we got a picture.
transcript.speaker: England
